hints.omnem: 'omnem' is an adjective meaning 'all/every' in the accusative
hints.regionem: 'regionem' is a noun meaning 'region' in the accusative

hints.Present: Present 'venit' looks identical — but the narrative context marks this as perfect
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'veniebat' — 'Venit' is 3rd singular perfect
hints.Future: Future would be 'veniet' — 'Venit' is 3rd singular perfect

hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the parts of 'videre' (to see)
hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the parts of 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.vendo, vendere, vendidi, venditum: These are the parts of 'vendere' (to sell)

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'regio' — 'regionem' with -em ending is accusative
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'regione' — 'regionem' is accusative after 'in' (motion into)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'regionis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'regionis' ends in -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'regionis' ends in -is

hints.Nominative: Nominative could be 'Iordanis' (same form), but here it modifies 'regionem' — genitive
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'Iordani' — 'Iordanis' is genitive (the region of the Jordan)
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'Iordane' — 'Iordanis' is genitive singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'Iordanis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'Iordanis' ends in -is
